enrichment.short_description: Skool is an all-in-one community platform that combines discussion feeds, courses, events, and gamified leaderboards so creators and businesses can run a paid or free community from a single link.
enrichment.pricing_model: Flat monthly or yearly subscription plus a transaction fee on paid community payments
enrichment.seo_title: Skool Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and How It Compares
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Skool's community and course platform works, what the Hobby and Pro plans cost, and how it stacks up against Circle and Mighty Networks in 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Skool is an online community platform launched in 2019 by founders Sam Ovens and Daniel Kang and headquartered in Los Angeles, California. It merges a social-feed-style community, structured online courses, live calls or events, and a gamified points and leveling system into one product, positioning it as an alternative to Circle, Mighty Networks, Discord, and Facebook Groups for creators who want to monetize an audience. Every Skool community lives at its own skool.com subdomain and includes unlimited members, unlimited courses, unlimited video hosting, and unlimited live calls regardless of plan tier. Skool charges a flat monthly fee (Hobby at $9 per month or Pro at $99 per month, both discounted on annual billing) plus a transaction fee on any paid membership revenue collected through the platform, which drops from 10 percent on Hobby to 2.9 percent on Pro. The platform's signature feature is its gamification layer: members earn points for posting and engaging, which unlocks levels and a leaderboard designed to drive daily habit formation inside a community. Skool is widely used by course creators, coaches, agencies, and info-product businesses running cohort-based programs or membership communities, and it has become a common alternative to hosting a community across separate Facebook Group, course platform, and email tools. As of 2026 the company reports LinkedIn headcount in the 11-50 employee range, though other estimates of contractor and remote headcount run higher.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Skool
paragraphs: Skool is a community and course platform built around a simple idea: put discussion, learning content, and engagement mechanics on one page so members do not have to jump between a Facebook Group, a course host, and a calendar tool. Every community gets a single feed, a classroom area for courses, a calendar for events, and a members directory, all under one custom URL.

Founded in 2019 by Sam Ovens and Daniel Kang, Skool grew quickly among online coaches, agency owners, and info-product creators who wanted a simpler alternative to Circle, Mighty Networks, Kajabi communities, or a bare Facebook Group paired with a separate course host. The company is based in Los Angeles and has expanded its team as usage has grown.

Skool is deliberately narrow in scope compared to broader community suites: there is no deep custom branding, no native email marketing, and no complex permission system. The trade-off is a fast, focused product that is easy for non-technical creators to set up and for members to actually use.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The core of Skool is its community feed, which works like a cross between a forum and a social network: members post, comment, and react, and the community owner can pin announcements or organize discussion by category.

Courses live inside the same community as modules and lessons with embedded video, so a creator can bundle a paid course and its discussion community into a single product instead of licensing a separate learning management system.

Skool's most distinctive feature is gamification: members earn points for participating, which move them up a level system and onto a public leaderboard. Community owners commonly unlock perks or recognition at higher levels to reward engagement.

Other included tools cover live calls and events with a shared calendar, an affiliate program that lets members earn a commission for referring new paying members, and a mobile app so communities stay active outside the browser.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Skool uses a flat two-tier pricing model rather than per-member pricing. The Hobby plan costs $9 per month and the Pro plan costs $99 per month, with roughly two months free on annual billing for either tier.

Both tiers include the same core feature set, unlimited members, unlimited courses, unlimited video, and unlimited live calls, so the meaningful difference between plans is the transaction fee Skool takes on paid membership revenue: 10 percent on Hobby versus 2.9 percent on Pro.

Because the fee is charged on revenue rather than the flat subscription, creators running a paid community with meaningful monthly recurring revenue tend to break even on the Pro plan quickly, while hobbyists or free-community owners can stay on Hobby indefinitely. A 14-day free trial is available before either plan is billed.

features.title: Unified community feed
features.description: A single social-style feed for posts, comments, and reactions replaces the need for a separate Facebook Group or forum.

features.title: Built-in courses
features.description: Modules and video lessons live inside the same space as the community, so a paid course and its discussion live together.

features.title: Gamified levels and leaderboard
features.description: Members earn points for activity that unlock levels and public leaderboard rank, encouraging daily engagement.

features.title: Live calls and events calendar
features.description: A shared calendar schedules live calls, workshops, and events directly inside the community.

features.title: Built-in affiliate program
features.description: Community owners can let members or partners earn a commission for referring new paying members.

features.title: Custom URL per community
features.description: Each community gets its own skool.com address that owners can promote as a single link.

features.title: Mobile app
features.description: iOS and Android apps let members post, chat, and watch course content outside the browser.

features.title: Simple payments and access control
features.description: Built-in checkout gates paid communities and courses without needing a separate payment integration.

pros: Single product covers community, courses, and events instead of stitching together three tools
Flat, predictable subscription pricing rather than per-member fees
Gamification (points, levels, leaderboard) drives measurably higher engagement than plain forums
Fast setup with a simple interface that is easy for non-technical creators and members alike
Built-in affiliate program helps creators grow paid membership without extra software
cons: Limited custom branding and design control compared to white-label community platforms
No native email marketing or automation, so creators often still need a separate email tool
Transaction fees on paid communities (2.9 percent to 10 percent) add up at scale on the wrong plan
Fewer granular permission and moderation controls than larger enterprise community platforms
No built-in native livestreaming; live calls rely on external tools like Zoom links

faqs.question: What is Skool used for
faqs.answer: Skool is used to run an online community that combines discussion, paid or free courses, live events, and member engagement in one place, commonly for coaching businesses, course creators, and membership programs.

faqs.question: How much does Skool cost
faqs.answer: Skool has two plans: Hobby at $9 per month with a 10 percent transaction fee on paid community revenue, and Pro at $99 per month with a 2.9 percent transaction fee. Annual billing gives roughly two months free.

faqs.question: Who founded Skool
faqs.answer: Skool was founded in 2019 by Sam Ovens and Daniel Kang and is headquartered in Los Angeles, California.

faqs.question: Does Skool have a free trial
faqs.answer: Yes, Skool offers a 14-day free trial before either the Hobby or Pro plan is billed.

faqs.question: How is Skool different from Circle or Mighty Networks
faqs.answer: Skool trades deep customization and branding for simplicity and a built-in gamification system (points, levels, and a leaderboard) that Circle and Mighty Networks do not offer natively.

faqs.question: Can you sell courses on Skool
faqs.answer: Yes, courses can be bundled with community access and sold through Skool's built-in checkout, with Skool taking a transaction fee based on the subscriber's plan.

faqs.question: Does Skool have a mobile app
faqs.answer: Yes, Skool has iOS and Android apps so members can browse the feed, message, and watch course content on mobile.

faqs.question: Does Skool support free communities
faqs.answer: Yes, community owners can run a completely free community and only pay the flat monthly subscription fee, since the transaction fee only applies to paid membership revenue.

use_cases.title: Paid coaching and mastermind communities
use_cases.description: Coaches bundle a private community, course content, and live group calls into one paid membership hosted on Skool.

use_cases.title: Course creator communities
use_cases.description: Creators sell a course alongside an ongoing discussion space so students stay engaged and accountable after purchase.

use_cases.title: Free brand or fan communities
use_cases.description: Businesses run a free community to build an engaged audience around a brand, product, or content niche using the gamified feed.

pricing_plans.billing_cycle: monthly or yearly (2 months free yearly)
features: 10 percent transaction fee on paid revenue

pricing_plans.billing_cycle: monthly or yearly (2 months free yearly)

enrichment.short_description: Slab is a team knowledge base and wiki tool that pairs a clean collaborative editor with fast unified search and integrations across Slack, Google Drive, and GitHub.
enrichment.pricing_model: Per-user monthly subscription with a free tier for small teams
enrichment.seo_title: Slab Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Slab's knowledge base and wiki software works, what the Startup and Business plans cost, and how it compares to Notion and Confluence.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Slab is a team knowledge base and wiki platform founded in 2016 by Anvisha Pai, Chengyin Liu, and Jason Chen, and headquartered in San Francisco, California. The product is built around a clean, distraction-free document editor combined with a unified search engine that indexes content inside Slab as well as connected third-party tools such as Slack, Google Drive, GitHub, and Salesforce, so teams can search their whole knowledge stack from one place. Slab positions itself as a simpler, faster alternative to Confluence and a more structure-focused alternative to general-purpose workspace tools like Notion, aiming specifically at documentation, onboarding materials, internal wikis, and process documents rather than project management or databases. Pricing runs on a per-user model: a Free plan covers up to 10 users with core editing and search, a Startup plan starts at $6.67 per user per month billed annually, a Business plan runs $12.50 per user per month billed annually and adds AI features (Autofix, Predict, and Ask), SAML SSO, SCIM provisioning, and a GraphQL API, and an Enterprise plan offers custom pricing for organizations with 100 or more users. All paid plans carry a 30-day money-back guarantee. Slab integrates with dozens of workplace tools and is commonly adopted by engineering, product, and operations teams that want a lightweight, well-organized internal wiki without the overhead of a heavier enterprise content platform.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Slab
paragraphs: Slab is a knowledge base and internal wiki tool designed to give teams a single, well-organized home for documentation, onboarding guides, meeting notes, and process docs. It was founded in 2016 by Anvisha Pai, Chengyin Liu, and Jason Chen and is based in San Francisco.

The product's core pitch is speed and clarity: a minimal editor that avoids the clutter of block-based, do-everything workspace tools, paired with search fast enough to make Slab a genuine first stop when someone needs an answer rather than a place documentation goes to become outdated.

Slab is commonly chosen by engineering, product, design, and operations teams who tried a general-purpose tool like Notion or a heavier platform like Confluence and wanted something narrower and more purpose-built for a team wiki.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Slab's editor supports rich text, code blocks, embeds, and templates, with real-time collaborative editing so multiple people can work on a document simultaneously.

Unified search is Slab's standout feature: it indexes not just Slab's own posts but connected tools like Slack, Google Drive, GitHub, and Salesforce, so a single search bar can surface an answer no matter where it originally lived.

Organization is handled through topics (Slab's equivalent of folders or spaces) that can be public or private, along with a 90 to 365-day version history depending on plan so teams can track how documents evolved.

On higher tiers, Slab adds AI-assisted writing tools (Autofix for grammar and formatting, Predict for auto-complete, and Ask for question-answering over the knowledge base), plus enterprise controls like SAML single sign-on, SCIM user provisioning, custom domains, and a GraphQL API for building custom integrations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Slab's Free plan supports up to 10 users plus a small number of guests per user, with 10MB attachment limits and 90 days of version history, making it usable for very small teams evaluating the product.

The Startup plan costs $6.67 per user per month billed annually (roughly $40/month billed monthly for the first block of users) and removes the user cap while adding private topics, larger attachments, and longer version history.

The Business plan costs $12.50 per user per month billed annually and adds the AI feature set, unlimited version history, custom domains, SAML SSO, SCIM provisioning, and priority support with an SLA. Enterprise pricing is custom and requires a minimum of 100 users, with dedicated support and fully customizable terms. Every paid plan includes a 30-day money-back guarantee.

features.title: Distraction-free collaborative editor
features.description: A clean rich-text editor with templates and real-time multi-user editing keeps documentation writing fast.

features.title: Unified search across integrations
features.description: Search indexes Slab content alongside connected tools like Slack, Google Drive, and GitHub from one search bar.

features.title: Topics for organization
features.description: Public or private topics group related documents the way folders or spaces would in other tools.

features.title: Version history
features.description: Every edit is tracked, with retention ranging from 90 days on lower tiers to unlimited history on Business.

features.title: AI writing and Q and A tools
features.description: Business-tier AI features include Autofix for cleanup, Predict for auto-complete, and Ask for answering questions from the knowledge base.

features.title: Enterprise security controls
features.description: SAML SSO and SCIM provisioning on the Business and Enterprise plans support centralized identity management.

features.title: Analytics
features.description: Usage analytics show which documents are viewed, helping teams spot outdated or missing documentation.

features.title: GraphQL API and webhooks
features.description: Business-tier API access lets teams build custom integrations or sync Slab content into other systems.

pros: Fast, focused search that spans Slab plus connected tools like Slack and Google Drive
Clean editor that is quick to learn compared to heavier block-based tools
Free plan is genuinely usable for teams up to 10 users
AI features (Autofix, Predict, Ask) are included at a mid-tier price point
30-day money-back guarantee reduces risk when adopting a paid plan
cons: Narrower feature set than all-in-one workspace tools like Notion (no databases, no task boards)
Free and Startup tiers cap attachment size and version history
Mobile experience is reported as weaker than the desktop app
Enterprise plan requires a 100-user minimum, which excludes smaller organizations from custom terms
Fewer third-party integrations than larger platforms like Confluence

faqs.question: What is Slab used for
faqs.answer: Slab is used as a team knowledge base and internal wiki for documentation, onboarding guides, meeting notes, and process docs.

faqs.question: How much does Slab cost
faqs.answer: Slab has a free plan for up to 10 users, a Startup plan from $6.67/user/month billed annually, a Business plan at $12.50/user/month billed annually, and custom Enterprise pricing for 100-plus users.

faqs.question: Who founded Slab
faqs.answer: Slab was founded in 2016 by Anvisha Pai, Chengyin Liu, and Jason Chen, and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

faqs.question: Does Slab have AI features
faqs.answer: Yes, the Business plan includes AI tools called Autofix, Predict, and Ask for writing assistance and question-answering over the knowledge base.

faqs.question: How is Slab different from Notion
faqs.answer: Slab focuses specifically on knowledge base and wiki use cases with unified cross-tool search, while Notion is a broader workspace tool with databases, task boards, and more flexible page structures.

faqs.question: How is Slab different from Confluence
faqs.answer: Slab offers a simpler, faster editor and search experience aimed at smaller and mid-size teams, while Confluence targets larger enterprises with deeper administrative and permission controls.

faqs.question: Does Slab integrate with Slack
faqs.answer: Yes, Slab integrates with Slack both for search indexing and for posting or sharing documents directly in Slack channels.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for Slab's paid plans
faqs.answer: Slab's paid plans come with a 30-day money-back guarantee rather than a separate trial period, letting teams try a paid tier risk-free.

use_cases.title: Engineering documentation
use_cases.description: Engineering teams document architecture decisions, runbooks, and onboarding guides in a searchable central wiki.

use_cases.title: Company-wide onboarding hub
use_cases.description: HR and operations teams centralize new-hire onboarding materials, policies, and process docs in organized topics.

use_cases.title: Cross-tool knowledge search
use_cases.description: Teams already using Slack, Google Drive, and GitHub adopt Slab primarily for its unified search across all three.

pricing_plans.billing_cycle: billed annually (monthly billing available at a higher rate)

enrichment.short_description: Slack is a team messaging and collaboration platform organized around channels, direct messages, and huddles, now owned by Salesforce and widely used as the default chat tool for businesses.
enrichment.seo_title: Slack Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and How It Compares
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Slack's channels, huddles, and workflow tools work, what the Pro and Business+ plans cost, and how Slack compares to Microsoft Teams in 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Slack is a business messaging and collaboration platform founded in 2013 by Stewart Butterfield, Eric Costello, Cal Henderson, and Serguei Mourachov, growing out of an internal chat tool the team built for their earlier game studio, Tiny Speck, and its game Glitch. Slack launched publicly in 2014, went public via direct listing in 2019, and was acquired by Salesforce in a deal announced in December 2020 for approximately $27.7 billion, closing in July 2021; it now operates as a Salesforce product while remaining headquartered in San Francisco, California. Slack organizes team communication into channels (topic- or team-based chat rooms), direct and group messages, threads, and huddles (lightweight audio or video calls), and it supports thousands of third-party integrations plus its own Workflow Builder for automating routine processes without code. As of 2026 Slack's pricing includes a Free plan with a limited searchable message history, a Pro plan at $7.25 per user per month billed annually, a Business+ plan at $15 per user per month billed annually with SAML SSO and compliance exports, and an Enterprise Grid plan with custom pricing for large organizations needing multiple connected workspaces and advanced governance. Since the Salesforce acquisition, Slack has increasingly bundled generative AI features (Slack AI) for search summarization, channel recaps, and huddle notes into its higher tiers. Slack remains one of the most widely adopted business chat tools globally, competing most directly with Microsoft Teams and Google Chat.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Slack
paragraphs: Slack is a channel-based messaging platform that became the default real-time communication tool for a generation of software and remote-first companies. It was created in 2013 by Stewart Butterfield, Eric Costello, Cal Henderson, and Serguei Mourachov, initially as an internal tool for their gaming company Tiny Speck before being spun out as a standalone product.

Slack launched publicly in February 2014 and grew rapidly by combining searchable, persistent chat channels with deep third-party integrations, replacing much of the day-to-day internal email traffic at many companies. The company went public via a direct listing on the NYSE in 2019.

In December 2020, Salesforce announced it would acquire Slack for roughly $27.7 billion, one of the largest software acquisitions at the time; the deal closed in July 2021 and Slack now operates as part of Salesforce while continuing to be headquartered in San Francisco.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Slack organizes conversation into channels that can be public, private, or shared across organizations, along with direct messages, group messages, and threaded replies to keep discussions organized within busy channels.
Huddles provide lightweight, low-friction audio or video calls launched directly from a channel or DM, positioned as a faster alternative to scheduling a formal video meeting.
Slack supports thousands of app integrations through its App Directory, plus Workflow Builder, a no-code tool for automating routine tasks like intake forms, approvals, and standups.

Since joining Salesforce, Slack has added Slack AI features on paid plans, including AI-generated channel recaps, thread summaries, and search answers that pull from an organization's message history.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Slack's Free plan supports unlimited users with a limited, rolling searchable message history and basic one-to-one voice and video calls, making it usable for very small teams or trial use.
The Pro plan costs $7.25 per user per month billed annually (higher on monthly billing) and unlocks unlimited message history, group calls, and unlimited app integrations.

The Business+ plan costs $15 per user per month billed annually and adds SAML-based single sign-on, compliance exports, and 99.99 percent guaranteed uptime, aimed at larger or more compliance-sensitive organizations.

Enterprise Grid, Slack's top tier, uses custom pricing (commonly cited in the low-to-high $20s per user per month depending on scale and negotiation) and is built for large enterprises that need multiple connected workspaces, org-wide search, and advanced data governance and security controls.

features.title: Channels
features.description: Public, private, and shared channels organize conversation by team, project, or topic instead of one continuous inbox.

features.title: Threads
features.description: Replies can branch into threads to keep side discussions from cluttering the main channel feed.

features.title: Huddles
features.description: One-click lightweight audio or video calls launch directly from a channel or direct message.

features.title: Workflow Builder
features.description: A no-code automation tool builds forms, approvals, and recurring reminders without custom development.

features.title: App integrations
features.description: Thousands of third-party apps connect to Slack through its App Directory, from Google Drive to Salesforce.

features.title: Slack AI
features.description: AI-powered channel recaps, thread summaries, and search answers are available on qualifying paid plans.

features.title: Enterprise Grid workspaces
features.description: Large organizations can connect multiple Slack workspaces under one Enterprise Grid with shared governance.

features.title: Voice and video calling
features.description: Built-in calling supports one-to-one calls on Free and group calls on paid plans.

pros: Widely adopted with the largest third-party app and integration ecosystem among team chat tools
Channel and thread structure scales well for large, distributed organizations
Huddles reduce meeting friction compared to scheduling a formal video call
Enterprise Grid offers mature governance and security controls for large, regulated companies
Deep integration with the broader Salesforce and Slack AI product suite since the acquisition
cons: Free plan's message history limit pushes most active teams toward a paid tier quickly
Per-user pricing gets expensive fast for large organizations, especially after 2025's Business+ price increase
Notification and channel overload is a common complaint in large, busy workspaces
Business+ and Enterprise Grid pricing has trended upward since the Salesforce acquisition
Some users prefer Microsoft Teams when already standardized on Microsoft 365

faqs.question: What is Slack used for
faqs.answer: Slack is used for real-time team messaging, file sharing, and workflow automation, organized into topic- or team-based channels rather than a single email-style inbox.

faqs.question: When was Slack founded
faqs.answer: Slack was founded in 2013 by Stewart Butterfield, Eric Costello, Cal Henderson, and Serguei Mourachov, and launched publicly in February 2014.

faqs.question: Who owns Slack
faqs.answer: Slack is owned by Salesforce, which announced its acquisition of Slack in December 2020 for approximately $27.7 billion and closed the deal in July 2021.

faqs.question: How much does Slack cost
faqs.answer: Slack has a free plan, a Pro plan at $7.25 per user per month billed annually, a Business+ plan at $15 per user per month billed annually, and custom Enterprise Grid pricing.

faqs.question: What is a Slack huddle
faqs.answer: A huddle is a lightweight audio or video call that can be started instantly from any channel or direct message without scheduling a formal meeting.

faqs.question: Does Slack have AI features
faqs.answer: Yes, Slack AI provides channel recaps, thread summaries, and AI-powered search answers on qualifying paid plans.

faqs.question: How is Slack different from Microsoft Teams
faqs.answer: Slack is generally seen as having a larger third-party integration ecosystem and a more chat-first interface, while Microsoft Teams is more tightly bundled with Microsoft 365 and Office apps.

faqs.question: Does Slack limit message history on the free plan
faqs.answer: Yes, the Free plan retains a limited, rolling window of searchable message history; paid plans unlock unlimited history.

use_cases.title: Company-wide team communication
use_cases.description: Organizations replace much of their internal email with channel-based messaging for day-to-day team communication.

use_cases.title: Cross-company collaboration
use_cases.description: Shared channels let businesses collaborate directly with external partners, vendors, or clients inside Slack.

use_cases.title: Workflow and process automation
use_cases.description: Teams use Workflow Builder to automate intake forms, approvals, and recurring status updates without custom code.

enrichment.short_description: SlashID is an identity security platform that gives developers unified detection, response, and governance across human and non-human identities in cloud, SaaS, and on-premises environments.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium usage-based subscription priced by monthly active users
enrichment.starting_price: Free (paid plans from $99/month for up to 10 monthly active users)
enrichment.seo_title: SlashID Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how SlashID's identity detection and response platform works, what it costs, and how it compares to other identity security tools for developers.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SlashID is an identity security platform founded in 2022 by Vincenzo Iozzo and Giovanni Gola and headquartered in Chicago, Illinois. The company raised roughly 8.83 million dollars in a seed round in March 2022 from investors including Alven Capital Partners, DG Daiwa Ventures, Headline, Musha Ventures, and TQ Ventures, and has since raised additional funding, bringing total known funding to around 10.6 million dollars. SlashID's platform is built for developers and security teams and is composed of two main modules: SlashID Access, a serverless, HSM-backed identity management service for building passwordless authentication, multi-tenancy, role-based access control, SAML, and multi-factor authentication without months of custom engineering; and SlashID Gate, which adds authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and bot detection to APIs and internal workloads without code changes. On top of these, SlashID offers identity threat detection and response (ITDR) capabilities with more than 500 built-in detections for identity misuse and posture drift across human and non-human (machine, service account, API key) identities, automated remediation actions such as forcing MFA, suspending compromised accounts, and rotating credentials, real-time phishing protection, and AI-powered access reviews. SlashID integrates with over 100 services, including major cloud providers, identity platforms like Okta and Active Directory, and enterprise tools like Salesforce and Slack. Pricing includes a free tier for a limited number of monthly active users and unlimited storage, with paid plans starting at $99 per month for up to 10 monthly active users and custom enterprise pricing for larger deployments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SlashID
paragraphs: SlashID is an identity security platform aimed at developers and security teams that need to protect both human user accounts and non-human identities such as service accounts, API keys, and machine credentials. It was founded in 2022 by Vincenzo Iozzo and Giovanni Gola and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.

The company was seeded with roughly 8.83 million dollars in March 2022 from investors including Alven Capital Partners, DG Daiwa Ventures, Headline, Musha Ventures, and TQ Ventures, positioning it in the identity threat detection and response (ITDR) category alongside vendors focused on securing identity infrastructure rather than just managing logins.

SlashID differentiates itself by combining identity infrastructure (authentication and access management building blocks) with continuous detection and automated response, so the same platform that helps developers build login flows also monitors for identity misuse after deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: SlashID Access is a serverless, globally replicated, HSM-backed identity management module that lets developers implement passwordless authentication, multi-tenancy, one-click user invitations, role-based access control, SAML, and multi-factor authentication in hours rather than weeks of custom engineering.

SlashID Gate adds authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and CAPTCHA-less bot detection to APIs and workloads, and can enforce passkeys or single sign-on on internal applications without modifying application code.

The platform ships with more than 500 built-in detections covering identity misuse and posture drift across both human and non-human identities, paired with automated remediation actions like forcing multi-factor authentication, suspending compromised accounts, and rotating exposed credentials.

Additional capabilities include real-time, browser-based phishing protection and AI-powered access reviews, along with integrations across more than 100 services, including major cloud providers, identity platforms like Okta and Active Directory, and tools like Salesforce and Slack.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SlashID offers a free tier that covers a limited number of monthly active users along with unlimited storage, making it accessible for developers evaluating the platform or running small projects.
Paid plans start at $99 per month for up to 10 monthly active users, scaling from there as an organization's number of managed identities grows.

Larger organizations with more complex identity security and governance needs can contact SlashID for custom enterprise pricing that includes dedicated support and expanded detection and integration coverage.

features.title: Passwordless authentication
features.description: SlashID Access supports passkeys and other passwordless flows without months of custom authentication engineering.

features.title: Non-human identity coverage
features.description: Detections and governance extend to service accounts, API keys, and machine credentials, not just human user logins.

features.title: 500+ built-in detections
features.description: Pre-built detection rules flag identity misuse and posture drift across an organization's connected identity sources.

features.title: Automated remediation
features.description: Detected issues can trigger automatic responses like forcing MFA, suspending accounts, or rotating credentials.

features.title: API and workload protection
features.description: SlashID Gate adds authentication, rate limiting, and bot detection to APIs without code changes.

features.title: Real-time phishing protection
features.description: Browser-based detection flags and blocks phishing attempts targeting employee identities in real time.

features.title: AI-powered access reviews
features.description: AI assists with periodic access reviews to flag excessive or stale permissions across identities.

features.title: Broad integrations
features.description: Connects with 100-plus services including cloud providers, Okta, Active Directory, Salesforce, and Slack.

pros: Covers both human and non-human identities in a single platform
Combines identity infrastructure (auth/access) with detection and response rather than requiring separate tools
Serverless, HSM-backed architecture is designed for fast implementation
Automated remediation reduces manual incident response time
Free tier makes it easy for developers to evaluate before committing to a paid plan
cons: Newer company (founded 2022) with a smaller track record than legacy identity vendors
Dashboard functionality reportedly lags behind what is available via the API, per user reviews
Best suited for organizations with in-house engineering resources to implement Gate and Access
Small team size (11-50 employees) may mean slower feature velocity than larger identity vendors
Enterprise pricing is custom and not published, requiring a sales conversation for larger deployments

faqs.question: What is SlashID used for
faqs.answer: SlashID is used for identity security, combining authentication and access management infrastructure with continuous detection and automated response for both human and non-human identities.

faqs.question: Who founded SlashID
faqs.answer: SlashID was founded in 2022 by Vincenzo Iozzo and Giovanni Gola and is headquartered in Chicago, Illinois.

faqs.question: How much does SlashID cost
faqs.answer: SlashID has a free tier for a limited number of monthly active users, with paid plans starting at $99 per month for up to 10 monthly active users and custom enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: What is SlashID Gate
faqs.answer: SlashID Gate adds authentication, authorization, rate limiting, and bot detection to APIs and internal workloads, and can enforce passkeys or SSO without code changes.

faqs.question: What is SlashID Access
faqs.answer: SlashID Access is a serverless, HSM-backed identity management module for building passwordless authentication, multi-tenancy, and role-based access control quickly.

faqs.question: Does SlashID protect non-human identities
faqs.answer: Yes, SlashID's detections and governance extend to service accounts, API keys, and other machine or non-human identities, not just human user accounts.

faqs.question: Does SlashID integrate with Okta or Active Directory
faqs.answer: Yes, SlashID integrates with over 100 services including Okta, Active Directory, major cloud providers, Salesforce, and Slack.

faqs.question: Is SlashID good for identity threat detection and response (ITDR)
faqs.answer: Yes, SlashID includes more than 500 built-in detections for identity misuse and posture drift along with automated remediation actions, placing it in the ITDR category.

use_cases.title: Developer-built authentication
use_cases.description: Engineering teams use SlashID Access to add passwordless login, multi-tenancy, and RBAC to a product without building identity infrastructure from scratch.

use_cases.title: Identity threat detection and response
use_cases.description: Security teams monitor for identity misuse and posture drift across cloud and SaaS identities, with automated remediation for detected threats.

use_cases.title: API and workload protection
use_cases.description: Platform teams use SlashID Gate to add authentication, rate limiting, and bot detection to internal and external APIs.

features: Up to 10 monthly active users included

enrichment.short_description: SleekFlow is an omnichannel conversational AI and social commerce platform that unifies WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, WeChat, Line, and other messaging channels into one sales and support inbox.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium subscription priced per user and monthly active contacts
enrichment.headquarters: Singapore (with offices in Hong Kong and other Asian markets)
enrichment.seo_title: SleekFlow Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how SleekFlow's omnichannel WhatsApp and social commerce platform works, what its plans cost, and who it is built for in 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: SleekFlow is an omnichannel social commerce and conversational AI platform that launched its beta in November 2019 and was incubated at Hong Kong's Cyberport before expanding its headquarters presence into Singapore alongside offices in Hong Kong, Malaysia, Indonesia, Brazil, and the UAE. The company was founded by Henson Tsai and has raised approximately 23.5 million dollars in funding from investors including Tiger Global and Atinum Investment. SleekFlow's core product unifies messaging channels including WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, Instagram, WeChat, Line, SMS, and live chat into a single shared inbox, letting sales and support teams manage full conversational journeys with a customer across channels without losing context. The platform layers in a visual Flow Builder for chatbot and automation logic, AI-assisted replies, broadcast campaigns, and commerce features such as product catalogs and in-chat checkout, positioning it particularly strongly in WhatsApp-first markets across Asia, the Middle East, and Latin America. SleekFlow reports being trusted by over 2,000 enterprises across 70 countries and processing tens of millions of messages per month. Pricing runs on a per-user, per-month-active-contact model: a Free plan supports 3 user accounts and roughly 50 monthly active contacts, a Pro AI plan starts at HK$399 per user per month (three-user minimum), a Premium AI plan starts at HK$579 per user per month (five-user minimum), and an Enterprise plan offers custom pricing with unlimited features. Annual billing saves up to 25 percent versus monthly billing.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is SleekFlow
paragraphs: SleekFlow is an omnichannel messaging and social commerce platform that brings conversations from WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, WeChat, Line, SMS, and live chat into a single shared inbox for sales and support teams. The company launched its beta in November 2019 after its founder, Henson Tsai, interviewed hundreds of prospective users, and it was incubated at Hong Kong's Cyberport.

SleekFlow has since expanded across Asia and beyond, with a presence spanning Singapore, Hong Kong, Malaysia, Indonesia, Brazil, and the UAE, and describes itself today as an agentic AI platform for turning conversations into sales. The company has raised roughly 23.5 million dollars from investors including Tiger Global and Atinum Investment.

The platform is particularly strong in WhatsApp-centric markets, where businesses rely heavily on messaging apps rather than email for customer communication, and SleekFlow reports serving over 2,000 enterprise customers across 70 countries.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: A unified inbox brings conversations from WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, WeChat, Line, SMS, and live chat into one place, so agents keep full context on a customer regardless of which channel they messaged from.
A visual Flow Builder lets teams design chatbot and automation logic without code, handling routine questions, lead qualification, and routing before a conversation reaches a human agent.
AI features assist with drafting replies, summarizing conversations, and increasingly automating parts of the sales journey, reflecting SleekFlow's positioning as an agentic AI platform.

Commerce tools include product catalogs and in-chat checkout so businesses can sell directly inside a WhatsApp or Messenger conversation, along with broadcast campaign tools for outbound marketing messages and CRM-style contact management.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: SleekFlow's Free plan includes 3 user accounts and roughly 50 monthly active contacts, giving small teams a way to trial the unified inbox and core automation features.

The Pro AI plan starts at HK$399 per user per month with a three-user minimum (roughly HK$1,199/month total), and additional users can be added at HK$199 per month each; it supports around 500 monthly active contacts and up.

The Premium AI plan starts at HK$579 per user per month with a five-user minimum (roughly HK$2,859/month total) and supports around 1,000 monthly active contacts and up, adding advanced analytics, API access, and role-based permissions.

An Enterprise plan offers custom pricing with unlimited usage tiers, custom integrations, and dedicated support, available by booking a demo. Annual billing saves up to 25 percent compared to monthly billing across paid tiers.

features.title: Unified omnichannel inbox
features.description: Conversations from WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, WeChat, Line, SMS, and live chat are combined into one inbox view.

features.title: Visual Flow Builder
features.description: A no-code automation builder handles chatbot logic, lead qualification, and conversation routing.

features.title: AI-assisted replies
features.description: AI helps agents draft responses and summarize conversations to speed up support and sales workflows.

features.title: In-chat commerce
features.description: Product catalogs and checkout let customers browse and buy directly inside a messaging conversation.

features.title: Broadcast campaigns
features.description: Outbound messaging campaigns reach segmented contact lists across supported channels.

features.title: Contact and CRM management
features.description: Built-in contact records track customer history and monthly active contact engagement across channels.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Premium and Enterprise tiers add permission controls for larger sales and support teams.

features.title: API access and integrations
features.description: Higher tiers include API access for connecting SleekFlow to external CRM, ecommerce, or analytics systems.

pros: Strong WhatsApp-first feature set built for markets where messaging apps dominate customer communication
Single inbox genuinely unifies many major messaging channels rather than a subset
In-chat commerce features support direct selling without leaving the conversation
Free plan and multiple paid tiers make it accessible from small teams to large enterprises
Backed by well-known investors (Tiger Global, Atinum Investment) supporting continued development
cons: Pricing is denominated in Hong Kong dollars, adding currency conversion friction for some international buyers
Paid plans have minimum user counts (3 for Pro AI, 5 for Premium AI), raising the effective entry price
Monthly active contact limits mean costs can scale quickly for high-volume messaging businesses
Headquarters and legal entity details vary across sources, reflecting the company's multi-country footprint
Company size and headcount are not publicly disclosed

faqs.question: What is SleekFlow used for
faqs.answer: SleekFlow is used to manage customer conversations and sales across WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, WeChat, Line, SMS, and live chat from one unified inbox.

faqs.question: When was SleekFlow founded
faqs.answer: SleekFlow launched its beta in November 2019, founded by Henson Tsai and incubated at Hong Kong's Cyberport.

faqs.question: How much does SleekFlow cost
faqs.answer: SleekFlow has a free plan for 3 users, a Pro AI plan starting at HK$399/user/month, a Premium AI plan starting at HK$579/user/month, and custom Enterprise pricing.

faqs.question: Does SleekFlow support WhatsApp
faqs.answer: Yes, WhatsApp is one of SleekFlow's primary supported channels, alongside Messenger, Instagram, WeChat, Line, SMS, and live chat.

faqs.question: Does SleekFlow have chatbot automation
faqs.answer: Yes, SleekFlow's visual Flow Builder lets teams create chatbot and automation logic without writing code.

faqs.question: Can you sell products directly in SleekFlow
faqs.answer: Yes, SleekFlow supports in-chat commerce with product catalogs and checkout inside a messaging conversation.

faqs.question: Who are SleekFlow's main competitors
faqs.answer: SleekFlow competes with other omnichannel and WhatsApp Business API platforms such as respond.io, Trengo, and Freshchat.

faqs.question: Where is SleekFlow headquartered
faqs.answer: SleekFlow is headquartered in Singapore with offices across Hong Kong, Malaysia, Indonesia, Brazil, and the UAE.

enrichment.short_description: Sleekplan is an all-in-one customer feedback platform combining a feedback board, public roadmap, changelog, and satisfaction surveys to help product teams turn user input into shipped features.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (Indie plan; paid plans from $13/month billed yearly)
enrichment.seo_title: Sleekplan Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Sleekplan's feedback board, roadmap, and changelog tool works, what the Starter and Business plans cost, and who it is built for.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Sleekplan is a bootstrapped, Munich-based customer feedback platform that began as an internal tool before becoming its founders' full-time focus starting in early 2020, with the company formally established in 2021. Sleekplan remains an independent, remote-first, EU-based company with a small team in the 2-10 employee range. The product covers the full feedback loop in one workspace: a feedback board where users submit and upvote ideas, a public roadmap that shows what is planned, in progress, and shipped, a changelog for announcing releases, and satisfaction surveys (CSAT and NPS) for measuring sentiment. A newer feature, Sleek Intelligence, uses AI to read incoming conversations and reviews, automatically extract feature requests and bug reports, file them, merge duplicates, and even auto-vote on existing items, reducing manual triage work for product teams. Sleekplan is priced on a per-workspace basis rather than strictly per user: the Indie plan is free forever with unlimited feedback and subscribers on a single-admin workspace; Starter costs $13 per month billed yearly and adds team collaboration for up to 3 seats plus 1,000 AI credits per month; Business costs $38 per month billed yearly (its most popular tier) and expands to 10 team seats, 5,000 AI credits, custom branding, and topic analytics; and Enterprise offers custom pricing with unlimited seats, SAML SSO, and multi-workspace support. All paid plans include a 30-day trial with no credit card required.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Sleekplan
paragraphs: Sleekplan is a customer feedback management platform that consolidates feedback collection, roadmapping, release announcements, and satisfaction surveys into a single tool. It grew out of an internal project that became its founders' full-time focus in early 2020, with the company formally founded in 2021 and based in Munich, Germany.

Unlike many feedback tools built by venture-backed teams, Sleekplan describes itself as bootstrapped and remote-first, run by a small team of roughly 2 to 10 people who ship product updates on a weekly cadence.

The platform is aimed at product teams and small-to-mid-size SaaS companies that want a lighter-weight alternative to enterprise feedback suites, letting customers submit ideas publicly, vote on what matters most, and see a transparent roadmap of what is planned or shipped.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The feedback board lets customers submit ideas, upvote existing requests, and discuss them in comments, giving product teams a ranked, crowdsourced backlog of user-requested improvements.

The public roadmap organizes submitted feedback into stages such as planned, in progress, and shipped, giving customers visibility into what the team is working on without needing separate status updates.
A built-in changelog lets teams announce new releases and link them back to the feedback items that inspired them, closing the loop for users who requested a feature.

Sleek Intelligence, an AI layer, reads incoming support conversations and reviews to automatically extract and file feature requests and bug reports, merge duplicate submissions, and vote on existing items on a user's behalf, reducing manual moderation work.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: The Indie plan is free forever and includes unlimited feedback and subscribers on a single-admin workspace with the core feedback board, changelog, and roadmap features.
The Starter plan costs $13 per month billed yearly and adds team collaboration for up to 3 seats, 1,000 AI credits per month, CSAT and NPS surveys, SSO, and API access.
The Business plan, Sleekplan's most popular tier, costs $38 per month billed yearly and expands to 10 team seats, 5,000 AI credits per month, topic analytics, and custom branding options.

The Enterprise plan uses custom pricing for large organizations, with unlimited team seats, SAML SSO, and multi-workspace support. Every paid plan includes a 30-day trial with no credit card required, and annual billing saves roughly two months compared to monthly billing.

features.title: Feedback board
features.description: Customers submit and upvote feature requests and bugs in a public or private board.

features.title: Public roadmap
features.description: Feedback items move through planned, in progress, and shipped stages visible to customers.

features.title: Changelog
features.description: Release announcements link back to the feedback that inspired them, closing the loop with requesters.

features.title: CSAT and NPS surveys
features.description: Built-in satisfaction surveys measure customer sentiment alongside feedback collection.

features.title: Sleek Intelligence AI triage
features.description: AI reads conversations and reviews to auto-file, de-duplicate, and vote on feedback items.

features.title: Topic analytics
features.description: Business-tier analytics surface trends across submitted feedback by topic or category.

features.title: SSO and API access
features.description: Starter and above include single sign-on and API access for integrating feedback data elsewhere.

features.title: Custom branding
features.description: Higher tiers let teams white-label the feedback board and roadmap to match their own brand.

pros: Covers the full feedback loop (board, roadmap, changelog, surveys) in one affordable tool
Free Indie plan is genuinely usable with unlimited feedback and subscribers
AI triage (Sleek Intelligence) reduces manual work filing and deduplicating requests
Transparent, EU-based bootstrapped company with a simple, predictable pricing structure
30-day trial with no credit card required lowers the barrier to testing paid tiers
cons: Small team (2-10 employees) means a narrower support and release capacity than larger competitors
Team seats are capped per tier (3 on Starter, 10 on Business), requiring Enterprise for larger teams
AI credits are metered per plan, which can limit heavy use of Sleek Intelligence on lower tiers
Less brand recognition than larger feedback tools like Canny or Productboard
Munich, EU-based operations may mean more limited US-hours support coverage

faqs.question: What is Sleekplan used for
faqs.answer: Sleekplan is used to collect customer feedback, manage a public product roadmap, announce releases via a changelog, and measure satisfaction with CSAT and NPS surveys.

faqs.question: How much does Sleekplan cost
faqs.answer: Sleekplan's Indie plan is free forever; Starter costs $13/month billed yearly, Business costs $38/month billed yearly, and Enterprise is custom priced.

faqs.question: Where is Sleekplan based
faqs.answer: Sleekplan is a bootstrapped, remote-first company headquartered in Munich, Germany, founded in 2021.

faqs.question: Does Sleekplan have AI features
faqs.answer: Yes, Sleek Intelligence uses AI to read conversations and reviews, automatically file feature requests and bugs, merge duplicates, and auto-vote on items.

faqs.question: Does Sleekplan offer a free trial
faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans include a 30-day trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Can customers vote on feature requests in Sleekplan
faqs.answer: Yes, the feedback board lets customers upvote and comment on submitted ideas to help teams prioritize.

faqs.question: Does Sleekplan support single sign-on
faqs.answer: Yes, SSO is included starting on the Starter plan.

faqs.question: How is Sleekplan different from Canny
faqs.answer: Sleekplan bundles feedback, roadmap, changelog, and surveys with AI-assisted triage at a generally lower price point than Canny, though Canny has broader brand recognition.

enrichment.short_description: Sling is employee scheduling and time tracking software that helps shift-based businesses build schedules, track hours, manage labor costs, and communicate with staff from one platform.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (up to 30 users); paid plans from $2/user/month
enrichment.headquarters: New York, New York, United States (subsidiary of Toast, Inc.)
enrichment.seo_title: Sling Review 2026: Pricing, Features, and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See how Sling's employee scheduling and time tracking software works, what the Premium and Business plans cost, and who it is built for in 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Sling is employee scheduling and workforce management software founded in 2015 by CEO Helgi Hermannsson, originally started in Reykjavik, Iceland, before the company expanded to the United States with a base in New York City. In July 2022, Sling was acquired by Toast, the publicly traded restaurant technology platform, and today operates as part of Toast's broader suite while continuing to serve businesses across industries beyond restaurants. Sling is built for businesses that schedule hourly, shift-based employees, offering drag-and-drop schedule building, availability and time-off management, labor cost forecasting, time tracking with geofencing for clock-ins, task management, and built-in team messaging so managers and staff can coordinate without a separate communication app. The product serves small businesses most heavily, with roughly 81 percent of reviewers on G2 coming from small companies, though it also supports midsize and enterprise customers, particularly in healthcare, fitness, retail, and hospitality. Sling's core scheduling features are free for up to 30 users, making it one of the more generous free tiers in the category, with paid plans starting at $2 per user per month for the Premium tier and going up to $4 per user per month for the Business tier, both offered with a free trial. Sling holds a 4.4-star rating on G2 based on reviews praising its ease of use, flexible shift swapping, and mobile access.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Sling
paragraphs: Sling is scheduling, time tracking, and team communication software built for businesses that manage hourly, shift-based employees. It was founded in 2015 by CEO Helgi Hermannsson, starting in Reykjavik, Iceland, before the company established a base in New York City to serve the US market.

In July 2022, Sling was acquired by Toast, the publicly traded restaurant technology company known for its point-of-sale platform, and Sling now operates as part of Toast's broader operations suite while still serving customers across healthcare, retail, fitness, hospitality, and other shift-based industries beyond restaurants.

Sling is positioned primarily for small businesses, though it scales into midsize and some enterprise use cases; roughly 81 percent of its G2 reviewers come from small companies, with health, wellness, and fitness businesses representing one of its largest customer segments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Managers build schedules with a drag-and-drop calendar and reusable templates, which speeds up building recurring weekly schedules for shift-based teams.
Employees can view schedules, request time off, set availability, and swap shifts with coworkers directly in the app, reducing the back-and-forth managers typically handle by text or phone.

Time tracking includes clock-in and clock-out with geofencing, so managers can confirm staff are clocking in from the correct work location, along with timesheets that feed into payroll and labor cost reports.

Built-in team messaging and task management let managers assign to-dos and communicate directly with staff, and labor cost forecasting tools help managers build schedules that stay within budget in real time as shifts are assigned.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Sling's core scheduling features are free for businesses with up to 30 users, covering schedule building, time-off requests, availability management, and basic messaging.
The Premium plan costs $2 per user per month and adds features like advanced time tracking, task management, and additional reporting for teams that have outgrown the free tier.

The Business plan costs $4 per user per month and adds the most advanced labor cost management, forecasting, and reporting tools for larger or more complex shift-based operations. A free trial is available before committing to either paid tier.

features.title: Drag-and-drop schedule builder
features.description: Managers build and adjust shift schedules visually with reusable templates for recurring weeks.

features.title: Shift swapping and availability
features.description: Employees can set availability, request time off, and swap shifts with coworkers inside the app.

features.title: Time tracking with geofencing
features.description: Clock-in and clock-out tracking confirms staff are on-site at the correct work location.

features.title: Labor cost forecasting
features.description: Real-time labor cost estimates help managers keep schedules within budget as shifts are assigned.

features.title: Team messaging
features.description: Built-in messaging lets managers communicate with staff without a separate chat app.

features.title: Task management
features.description: Managers assign and track to-do items alongside scheduling for daily operations.

features.title: Timesheets and payroll integration
features.description: Tracked hours feed into timesheets and can integrate with payroll systems.

features.title: Mobile apps
features.description: iOS and Android apps let employees view schedules, clock in, and message from their phone.

pros: Free tier supports up to 30 users, generous compared to most scheduling competitors
Drag-and-drop scheduling and shift swapping are widely praised for ease of use
Time tracking with geofencing adds accountability for on-site clock-ins
Built-in messaging removes the need for a separate team communication tool
Now backed by Toast, giving it more resources following the 2022 acquisition
cons: Deeper payroll and HR features generally require external integrations
Some advanced reporting is reserved for the Business tier rather than Premium
Company size and standalone revenue are not disclosed separately from parent company Toast
Primarily optimized for shift-based hourly work, less suited to salaried or project-based teams
Since the Toast acquisition, some users report a stronger product focus on food service and hospitality

faqs.question: What is Sling used for
faqs.answer: Sling is used for employee scheduling, time tracking, and team communication for businesses that manage hourly, shift-based staff.

faqs.question: How much does Sling cost
faqs.answer: Sling's core scheduling features are free for up to 30 users; the Premium plan costs $2 per user per month and the Business plan costs $4 per user per month.

faqs.question: Who owns Sling
faqs.answer: Sling was founded in 2015 by Helgi Hermannsson and was acquired by Toast, the restaurant technology company, in July 2022.

faqs.question: Does Sling track employee time
faqs.answer: Yes, Sling includes time tracking with clock-in and clock-out and geofencing to confirm employees are at the correct work location.

faqs.question: Can employees swap shifts in Sling
faqs.answer: Yes, employees can request time off, set availability, and swap shifts with coworkers directly in the app, subject to manager approval.

faqs.question: Is Sling only for restaurants
faqs.answer: No, Sling serves a range of shift-based industries including healthcare, retail, fitness, and hospitality, though it is now owned by restaurant technology company Toast.

faqs.question: Does Sling have a mobile app
faqs.answer: Yes, Sling has iOS and Android apps for managers and employees to manage schedules and time tracking on the go.

faqs.question: Does Sling integrate with payroll systems
faqs.answer: Yes, tracked hours and timesheets can be exported or integrated with external payroll systems.

use_cases.description: Clinics, gyms, and wellness businesses coordinate shift coverage and time-off requests for part-time and hourly staff.

use_cases.title: Labor cost management
use_cases.description: Operations managers use real-time labor cost forecasting to keep shift schedules within a weekly budget.

enrichment.short_description: Slink is a link-in-bio tool that lets creators and businesses build a single shareable landing page linking out to their social media, streaming, e-commerce, and website profiles.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium subscription with a paid Premium tier
enrichment.starting_price: Free (Premium tier available with a 14-day free trial)
enrichment.seo_title: Slink Review 2026: What It Is, Features, and Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: See what the Slink link-in-bio tool does, how its free and Premium plans compare, and how it stacks up against Linktree and similar bio-link tools.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Slink, also marketed as myslink.app, is a link-in-bio tool that lets creators, businesses, and individuals combine all of their social media, streaming, e-commerce, and website links into a single shareable landing page accessed through one custom URL. Users choose a unique Slink address and add platforms such as Instagram, Snapchat, Facebook, YouTube, or any other social network or website to their profile, then share that one link in places like an Instagram or TikTok bio where only a single outbound link is normally allowed. This puts Slink in the same product category as Linktree, Beacons, and similar bio-link and digital business card tools, which have become common infrastructure for creators, small businesses, and social sellers who need to direct followers to multiple destinations from one place. Slink offers a free tier for building a basic bio-link page and a Premium tier available with a 14-day free trial that unlocks additional customization or analytics features. Public information about Slink's founding date, headquarters, and company size is limited and not consistently confirmed across available sources, so prospective users evaluating the tool for long-term use should verify current company details and plan specifics directly on myslink.app before committing. Slink's core value proposition remains straightforward: reduce every creator's or business's online presence to one link that can be shared anywhere a single URL is permitted, then keep that page updated as new content or products go live.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is Slink
paragraphs: Slink (myslink.app) is a link-in-bio tool: a single, customizable landing page that consolidates all of a person's or brand's important links, social profiles, streaming platforms, and online store pages behind one shareable URL.

The core use case is solving the one-link limitation that platforms like Instagram and TikTok impose on bio links. Instead of choosing a single destination, a creator or business points their bio to their Slink page, which then fans out to every other destination they want to promote.

Slink sits in the same product category as Linktree and Beacons, competing on ease of setup, page customization, and price for creators, small businesses, and social sellers who need a lightweight alternative to a full website.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Users claim a unique Slink address and build a single page listing links to social profiles (Instagram, Snapchat, Facebook, YouTube, TikTok, and others), streaming platforms, e-commerce stores, and any other website.
The page functions as a lightweight digital business card as well as a bio-link hub, making it useful both for personal branding and for pointing customers to a product catalog or booking page.
A free tier covers the basic bio-link page setup, while a Premium tier, available with a 14-day free trial, adds further customization and features on top of the free experience.
Because the entire product is a single shareable page, setup is fast and does not require any code or design skills, which is the primary appeal versus building a full personal or business website.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Slink offers a free plan that lets users create a basic bio-link page and add their core set of social and website links at no cost.
A Premium tier is available for users who want additional customization or features beyond the free page, and it can be tried free for 14 days before any payment is required.
Exact Premium pricing is not consistently published across third-party sources, so users should confirm current pricing directly on myslink.app before subscribing.

features.title: Single custom link address
features.description: Users claim a unique Slink URL that becomes their one shareable link across social bios.

features.title: Unlimited outbound links
features.description: One Slink page can link out to social profiles, streaming platforms, stores, and websites.

features.title: Digital business card format
features.description: The page doubles as a simple digital business card for personal or brand identity.

features.title: Social platform support
features.description: Common integrations include Instagram, Snapchat, Facebook, YouTube, and other major social networks.

features.title: E-commerce and streaming links
features.description: Slink pages can point to online stores and streaming platform profiles alongside social links.

features.title: No-code setup
features.description: Pages are built without design or development skills, using a simple link-adding interface.

features.title: Free and Premium tiers
features.description: A free plan covers the basics, with a Premium tier available via a 14-day free trial.

pros: Fast, no-code setup for a single shareable bio-link page
Free tier is usable without a subscription for basic needs
Doubles as a lightweight digital business card, not just a link list
Supports a wide range of social, streaming, and e-commerce link types
14-day free trial lowers the risk of testing the Premium tier
cons: Public information on company background, founding date, and team size is limited and inconsistent across sources
Premium pricing is not clearly published in a consistent way across third-party listings
Smaller brand recognition than category leaders like Linktree or Beacons
Feature depth (analytics, e-commerce tools) is less documented than larger competitors
Prospective users should verify current features and pricing directly on the official site before committing

faqs.question: What is Slink
faqs.answer: Slink is a link-in-bio tool that lets users combine social media, streaming, e-commerce, and website links into one shareable landing page.

faqs.question: Is Slink free to use
faqs.answer: Yes, Slink offers a free tier for building a basic bio-link page, with a Premium tier available via a 14-day free trial for additional features.

faqs.question: How is Slink different from Linktree
faqs.answer: Slink serves the same core link-in-bio purpose as Linktree, combining multiple links into one page, though it has smaller brand recognition and less publicly documented feature depth.

faqs.question: Who is Slink for
faqs.answer: Slink is aimed at creators, small businesses, and social sellers who need one link to share across Instagram, TikTok, and other platforms that only allow a single bio link.

faqs.question: Does Slink support e-commerce links
faqs.answer: Yes, Slink pages can include links to online stores and product pages alongside social and streaming profiles.

faqs.question: Does Slink require coding skills to set up
faqs.answer: No, Slink pages are built through a simple no-code interface for adding and organizing links.

faqs.question: How much does Slink Premium cost
faqs.answer: Premium pricing is not consistently published across third-party sources; users should check current pricing directly on myslink.app.

use_cases.title: Creator bio-link page
use_cases.description: Content creators point their Instagram or TikTok bio to a single Slink page listing all their social and content links.

use_cases.title: Small business link hub
use_cases.description: Small businesses use a Slink page to direct customers to their store, booking page, and social profiles from one link.

use_cases.title: Personal digital business card
use_cases.description: Individuals use Slink as a lightweight digital business card to share contact and social links in one place.

features: Extended features beyond the free tier
